notes: Manual compiled first-pass strategic resources profile. Numeric production, export, reserve/resource and state-footprint rows are copied from the named official source envelopes; unavailable values stay unavailable.
source_rights: Project metadata/prose CC BY 4.0; upstream source rights remain with each publisher.
citation: Fuel Resilience AU strategic resources methodology; Department of Industry, Science and Resources; Geoscience Australia.

fields.method: Production, export value, export volume, Ore Reserves, Mineral Resources, state footprint and strategic role are separate fields. No underground-wealth total or combined mineral balance-sheet value is published.

takeaways.title: Iron ore is the largest loaded individual export-value row
takeaways.text: The loaded REQ workbook row reports iron ore export value of A$116.4b in 2024-25. Gold, metallurgical coal and thermal coal are also large loaded rows, but coal is shown as separate metallurgical and thermal rows rather than a single mixed number.

takeaways.title: Strategic does not mean one category
takeaways.text: The loaded strategic-role source ties critical minerals and strategic materials to net zero, advanced manufacturing, defence technologies and broader strategic applications. Iron ore, coal, gold and uranium add trade, industrial and energy-security roles outside the narrow critical-minerals list.

takeaways.title: Coverage is intentionally uneven
takeaways.text: Major bulk and metal commodities have production, export and state-footprint coverage. Rare earths have production/reserve and state-footprint coverage but no individual export-value row is loaded. Sulphur remains unavailable.

exports.summary: A$116.4b export value and 899.9 Mt export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 12,330 Mt at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: WA dominates loaded operating rows; SA, NT and TAS also have operating rows.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Operating-mine layer counts source feature rows, not tonnes by state.
resources.strategic_role_line: Core steelmaking feedstock and Australia's largest loaded strategic-resource export-value row.
resources.defence_relevance: Indirect: steel supply chains, infrastructure and heavy industry.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Enables grid, rail, port and renewable-energy infrastructure steel.
resources.manufacturing_relevance: Steelmaking input; downstream value depends on processing and steel capacity.
resources.trade_relevance: Largest loaded individual resource export-value row.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier and number one EDR holder for iron ore.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, export value, export volume, reserves/resources and state-footprint rows are all loaded.

production.summary: 424 Mt saleable black coal mine production in 2024.

exports.summary: Metallurgical coal: A$39.3b export value and 147.0 Mt export volume in 2024-25.
Thermal coal: A$32.0b export value and 205 Mt export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 10,959 Mt at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded operating rows are concentrated in QLD and NSW, with smaller counts in TAS, VIC and WA.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: The dashboard keeps metallurgical and thermal coal exports separate; the operating-mines layer does not split coal rows by market use.
resources.strategic_role_line: Steelmaking coal, export income and domestic energy-system legacy exposure.
resources.defence_relevance: Indirect: steel supply chains and energy-system resilience.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Transition exposure: thermal coal demand falls in decarbonisation pathways, while metallurgical coal remains tied to steel.
resources.trade_relevance: Two large export-value rows are loaded, but metallurgical and thermal coal are not collapsed into one number.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier of black coal.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, export and state-footprint context are loaded; coal sub-type production by state is not loaded.

exports.summary: A$1.17b export value and 5,034 t export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 307 kt U at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded uranium rows are in SA, with a developing row in WA.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Operating-mine feature counts are not uranium tonnes by state and do not include export-control approvals.
resources.strategic_role_line: Nuclear fuel supply with export controls and national-security sensitivity.
resources.defence_relevance: Strategic nuclear-fuel supply chain relevance; not a weapons claim.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Supports nuclear electricity fuel supply for importing countries.
resources.trade_relevance: Smaller export value than bulk commodities, but high strategic sensitivity.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier and number one EDR holder for uranium.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, export value, reserves and state footprint are loaded.

exports.summary: Bauxite: A$3.3b export value and 43.2 Mt export volume in 2024-25.
Alumina: A$12.2b export value and 14.7 Mt export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 1,783 Mt bauxite at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded bauxite mine rows are in QLD, WA and NT, with developing rows in QLD and TAS.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: State footprint is bauxite mine rows only; alumina refinery footprint is not counted here.
resources.strategic_role_line: Upstream aluminium supply chain for transport, grid hardware, defence and manufacturing.
resources.defence_relevance: Aluminium supply chain input for aerospace, vehicles and equipment.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Aluminium is used in transmission, vehicles, solar frames and lightweight structures.
resources.manufacturing_relevance: Bauxite and alumina feed aluminium smelting and fabrication.
resources.trade_relevance: Bauxite and alumina export rows are loaded separately.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier of bauxite.
resources.coverage_notes: Bauxite production/export and alumina export rows are loaded; alumina refinery state footprint is not loaded.

exports.summary: A$12.6b export value and 765.4 kt total metallic-content export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 19.11 Mt Cu at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded operating rows appear in NSW, WA, QLD and SA, with care/developing rows across several states.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Copper footprint includes source groups where copper is the lead commodity plus the mixed Cu/Au/U/Ag row.
resources.strategic_role_line: Electricity networks, data centres, motors, manufacturing and defence electronics.
resources.defence_relevance: Electrical systems, electronics and munitions supply chains.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: High relevance for transmission, renewables, electric motors and storage systems.
resources.manufacturing_relevance: Base input for wiring, equipment and industrial machinery.
resources.trade_relevance: Loaded export row includes ores/concentrates and refined copper metallic content.
resources.global_significance: DISR lists copper as an Australian strategic material.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, export value, reserve/resource and footprint rows are loaded.

resources.resource_type: Critical mineral / stainless steel and battery input

exports.summary: A$2.3b export value and 81.2 kt export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 2.6 Mt Ni at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded operating rows are in WA; many WA nickel rows are care and maintenance.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Feature rows show mine status, not market viability or production volume by site.
resources.strategic_role_line: Battery chemistries, stainless steel, alloys and defence supply chains, with current market weakness.
resources.defence_relevance: Alloys, stainless steel and battery-adjacent supply chains.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Battery and clean-technology input, depending on chemistry and processing pathway.
resources.manufacturing_relevance: Stainless steel, alloys and chemical processing.
resources.trade_relevance: Export row is loaded but REQ notes weaker prices and lower domestic production.
resources.global_significance: DISR lists nickel as a critical mineral.
resources.coverage_notes: Metric coverage is strong; market context is weak and explicitly caveated.

exports.summary: A$4.8b total export value in 2024-25; REQ separately lists spodumene and lithium hydroxide rows.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 4,601 kt Li at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded operating rows are in WA; NT appears as care and maintenance.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Lithium footprint uses Li/Ta operating-mines feature rows; it is not spodumene tonnage by mine.
resources.strategic_role_line: Battery supply chain input with strong export and processing-policy relevance.
resources.defence_relevance: Energy storage and portable power supply chains.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: High relevance for electric vehicles and battery energy storage.
resources.trade_relevance: REQ reports both spodumene and refined lithium product export rows.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier of lithium.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, export, reserve/resource and WA-heavy footprint rows are loaded.

resources.resource_type: Critical minerals / magnet and defence-relevant elements

exports.summary: Individual rare earth export value is not loaded; REQ reports an aggregate other-critical-minerals row that is not attributed here.
exports.notes: No individual rare earth export-value row is loaded.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 2.05 Mt oxide at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded rare-earth rows include one operating row in WA and developing rows in WA, NSW and NT.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Rare-earth footprint uses the Rare earth elements commodity group only; mineral sands with REE are not added to the headline count.
resources.strategic_role_line: Permanent magnets, defence systems, wind turbines, electric vehicles and supply-chain diversification.
resources.defence_relevance: High-performance magnets, sensors and advanced defence technologies.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Permanent magnets for wind turbines and electric vehicles.
resources.manufacturing_relevance: Processing and separation capability is strategically important.
resources.trade_relevance: Individual export value is unavailable in this first pass.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier of rare earths; the CMSR initial subset includes light and heavy rare earth elements.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, reserves/resources and footprint are loaded; individual export value remains unavailable.

resources.resource_type: Precious metal / reserve asset and export resource

exports.summary: A$46.9b export value and 239.1 t export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 3,369 t Au at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded operating rows are concentrated in WA, with operating rows also in VIC, QLD, NSW, NT, SA and TAS.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Precious-metals feature rows are not gold production tonnes by state.
resources.strategic_role_line: Major export row, financial reserve asset and electronics/manufacturing input.
resources.defence_relevance: Electronics and secure financial reserve relevance.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Limited direct energy-transition role; industrial electronics use is relevant.
resources.trade_relevance: Second-largest loaded individual export-value row on this page.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier and number one EDR holder for gold.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, export, reserve/resource and footprint rows are loaded.

exports.summary: A$4.4b export value and 1,264.1 kt total metallic-content export volume in 2024-25.

reserve_or_resource.summary: Ore Reserves: 15.56 Mt Zn at operating mines as at 31 Dec 2024.

resources.state_footprint_summary: Loaded operating rows are in QLD, NSW, NT and TAS, with developing/care rows in NSW, QLD, SA and WA.
resources.state_footprint_boundary: Zinc footprint uses Zn/Pb feature rows and is not a production allocation by state.
resources.strategic_role_line: Galvanising, alloys, batteries and industrial manufacturing supply chains.
resources.energy_transition_relevance: Grid and infrastructure galvanising; emerging battery chemistries.
resources.trade_relevance: Export row includes ores/concentrates and refined zinc metallic content.
resources.global_significance: AIMR 2025 release reports Australia as a top-five global supplier and number one EDR holder for zinc; DISR lists zinc as a strategic material.
resources.coverage_notes: Production, export, reserve/resource and footprint rows are loaded.

resources.resource_type: Potential industrial input / source gate only

headline_metric.notes: No official national sulphur production or export row is loaded.

production.notes: No official national sulphur production row is loaded.

exports.notes: No official national sulphur export row is loaded.

reserve_or_resource.summary: No official national reserve/resource row is loaded.

resources.state_footprint_boundary: No official/public state footprint source is loaded.
resources.strategic_role_line: Potential fertiliser and chemical input, but not source-safe in this first pass.
resources.manufacturing_relevance: Potential chemical and fertiliser supply-chain relevance, not quantified.
resources.coverage_notes: Left unavailable rather than guessing from weak or non-official sources.

strategic_relevance.summary: Defence relevance is framed as supply-chain input relevance: nuclear fuel export control, rare-earth magnets, electronics, alloys, corrosion protection and steel/aluminium inputs.
strategic_relevance.boundary: The page does not claim direct weapon use, classified demand or defence stockpile quantities.

strategic_relevance.summary: Energy-transition relevance covers batteries, permanent magnets, electricity networks, lightweight structures, galvanised grid hardware and nuclear fuel supply for importing countries.
strategic_relevance.boundary: Coal is treated as transition exposure rather than a transition mineral.

strategic_relevance.summary: Industrial relevance covers steel, aluminium, galvanising, alloys, wiring, equipment, electronics and processing value-add.
strategic_relevance.boundary: The dashboard does not infer downstream manufacturing value where the source only supports upstream mining or export rows.

strategic_relevance.summary: Trade relevance is shown with REQ export-value and export-volume rows where available. Rare earths and sulphur stay unavailable for individual export values.
strategic_relevance.boundary: Export values are not production and are not reserves/resources.
